1. 	Strictly adhere to the conditions stipulated in Annex A of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) before resorting to implement infrastructure projects by administration, to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit if found to be not in accordance with regulations.

2. 	Ensure that infrastructure projects by administration be given utmost priority in the utilization of agency’s owned heavy equipment, so as to minimize the cost of the project. 

3. 	Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation why he did not request an inspection of the project from the Office of the Auditor in order to attest the completeness and reasonableness of the project cost.
observations.managementAction: Acted by management but there is still remaining unrefunded amount.
observations.reason: Partial refund was made by the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation:
1. Create an Inventory Committee who shall be responsible for the conduct of annual physical count of the Agency’s PPEs. Likewise, assign property number and put a sticker for each PPE according to its classification.
 
2. Submit immediately to COA Audit Team the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the Accounting records, to establish the validity of the reported balances.  
                               3. Comply with the reglementary date of submission of the report which is not later than January 31, of every year as prescribed by the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The management had partially conducted an actual physical count in 2016.
observations.reason: Only 4 classification of PPEs  accounts have been reconciled with the records of the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and other accountable officers to submit the required accounting and financial reports within the prescribed deadlines and implement adequate measures that will enable the Agency to meet the deadlines in pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995, to ensure the timely review and verification of accounts on the financial position of the agency for the period.
 
2. Cause the automatic suspension of payment of the salaries of concerned personnel until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed under Section 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management has already submitted all their monthly reports in 2014.

observations.recommendation:
1. We have recommended to create and strengthen a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) who will undertake the tasks/functions related to gender issues and concerns so that it will fully address a gender responsive PPAs in the Municipality.
 
2.  To conduct Gender Analysis using the sex-disaggregated data/information from the LGU GAD Database to properly identify GAD issues in accordance with Section 4.1 of  PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
 
3. To formulate LGU GAD Code and database in order to prioritize gender issues that promote a gender responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had created a GFPS.

observations.recommendation:
1. We have recommended to the Agency to terminate the contract with Joint Venture of E.M. Paule Construction & Trading and POIEL Engineering and Management Services considering that the project was abandoned for almost a year, following all the procedures for termination of contract as specified in Section IV of General Conditions of Contract.
 
 
2. To institute legal action against the erring contractor and recommend the blacklisting/banning of the said contractor in participation to LGUs subsequent procurements.  
 
3.   To demand the immediate refund of the remaining un-recouped advances totaling         P115,684.20 or deduct the said amount from the retention money or any claims due from the concerned contractor.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has terminated the contract.
observations.reason: No document to attest that there was  blacklisting done to the contractor and no legal action was made and the advances to contractors was not yet fully recoup.

observations.recommendation:
1.  Ensure that the provisions in the MOA be adhered to.
 
2.  Submit the documentary requirements such as Program of Work, Request and/or Statement of Variation Order/Extra Work duly approved by DILG (the source agency), pictures of the project etc., to avoid suspension in audit.
 
3.  To submit justification as to why the sanctions stipulated in the MOA between the LGU and DILG could not be imposed to the LGU since there was a clear manifestation that proper supervision was not done.
observations.managementAction: The management had already submitted the required  documentary requirement

observations.recommendation:
Direct the erring collector, Ms. Annabel V. Akmad to restitute immediately the missing funds amounting P724, 717.71 and to produce the 35 missing Official Receipts (AF 51) with Serial No. 3326516-550 together with the corresponding collections made thereof the soonest possible time in order to recover the LGU funds and property of the municipality of Mawab found in audit.                            

Direct the Municipal Treasurer concerned to exercise efficient and effective supervision and control over all personnel issued with accountable forms to safeguard government funds from further loss and misapplication.  He should undertake necessary measures to strengthen internal controls in his Office.  Collectors found to be negligent in their jobs should be relieved immediately and be recommended for dismissal.
 
The various collectors should be required to make daily turn-over of their collections and the series of ORs issued by them should be closely monitored in order to avoid repetition of irregularities done by Ms. Akmad.  Further, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should find ways and means to ensure that the amount reflected in the originals are the same with those of the duplicates on a daily basis without inconveniencing the paying public.

If necessary, the concerned Municipal Treasurer should designate a liquidating officer from among his personnel to perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections and monitor the issuance of accountable forms of the various collectors.

Above all, the Municipal Treasurer concerned should be required to give written explanations why Ms. Annabel V. Akmad, the erring collector and being one of the personnel under his direct supervision committed said irregularities and the justifications why he should not be held liable for the 724,717.71 loss of government funds of the Municipality of Mawab.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had partially recovered part of the amount misappropriated.
observations.reason: There was a case filed with the concerned AO.

observations.recommendation:
Give utmost priority to the titling of the lands which are utilized by the municipality in order to ensure ownership over the said lands and to avoid possible damage to government properties by unwanted transfer through eviction.
 
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries in the books for erroneous recordings made on the land account such as the cost of structures-the Public Infra costing P2.5M which were recorded as land as well as the P500,000.00 down payment made on the lot intended for the new Public Terminal which should be recorded as Guaranty Deposits and not as Land.   

Further, the Municipal Accountant should revise the supporting schedule of the Land account.  The necessary information such as the TCT No., the location, and current use of the various lots owned by the Municipality should be stated under the "Particular" column of said schedule while the other lots owned by the municipality of which the titling of lots are still in process, should be mentioned in the Notes to FS.
observations.managementAction: The management made an appeal/request to the Office of the President of the Philippines for its proclamation.
observations.reason: Not all the land were titled under the LGUs name. They are still waiting for the proclamation of these titles duly signed by the President of the Philippines.
